steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence opens with the participial phrase "Established in 1936 by African American novelist Richard Wright". A participial phrase must modify the subject that immediately follows the comma—so that subject must be the thing Richard Wright established in 1936.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "the South Side Writers Group provided a valuable forum for Chicago writers to share ideas;": "The South Side Writers Group" is what Richard Wright established in 1936, so the participial phrase correctly modifies it. The semicolon then joins a second independent clause about what the group would become. ✓
  • "writers shared ideas at a valuable forum known as the South Side Writers Group in Chicago;": The subject is "writers"; Richard Wright did not establish "writers" in 1936—dangling modifier. ✗
  • "Chicago was where the South Side Writers Group provided a valuable forum for writers to share ideas;": The subject is "Chicago"; Richard Wright did not establish Chicago in 1936—dangling modifier. ✗
  • "Chicago writers in the South Side Writers Group had a valuable forum for sharing ideas;": The subject is "Chicago writers"; Richard Wright did not establish "Chicago writers" in 1936—dangling modifier. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
